value:
The Flüela station is part of the Swiss Permafrost Monitoring Network (PERMOS; www.permos.ch). The station is important to monitor and analyse the evolution of ground temperature, permafrost thickness, and the degradation of ground ice in talus deposits, which are important forms of debris storage in mountain environments and can cause mass movements, subsidence or instability of mountain infrastructure. It is the first permafrost research site of the Swiss Alps (Haeberli 1975).
Two boreholes were drilled in 2002, instrumented with thermistor chains, one being still in use with ground temperatures measured down to 25 m. 
The station also delivers data to the Swiss Inter cantonal Measurement and Information System (IMIS) network used by the Swiss Avalanche Warning Service WSL/SLF (https://whiterisk.ch/fr/conditions/measurements/station/IMIS/FLU2).
_
Additional cryosphere measurements:
- Snow (gentle grassy slope):
Snow surface temperature
Snow temperatures at 25, 50, and 100 cm above ground (from 2003 to 2020)
- Permafrost (talus slope):
Daily photographs of site and regular high resolution aerial photographs;
Electrical Resistance Tomography (ERT) and seismic measurements were done in 1975 and from 2009 to 2012.
_

value: Last ozone observing instruments (1 Brewer and 1 Dobson) transferred to Davos (0-20008-0-DAV). The ozone observations continues from there.
candidateValues: Last ozone observing instruments (1 Brewer and 1 Dobson) transferred to Davos (0-20008-0-DAV). The ozone observations continues from there.

value: The ozone observations from Arosa have been transferred to Davos (0-20008-0-DAV). Arosa which is approximately 12 km south-west of Davos and about 200 m higher. The parallel observation period (with instruments of Arosa being temporarily deployed in Davos) started on 22 Nov 2011. 4 of the 6 instruments (2 Brewers 2 Dobsons) were definitively transferred to Davos on 28 Feb 2018. The two remaining instruments (1 Brewer and 1 Dobson) continued observations in Arosa until 18 Feb 2021 when they were also transferred to Davos and the ozone observations at Arosa definitively stopped. Since then, the ozone time series is continued from Davos.

value: The ozone observations which started in Arosa (0-20008-0-ARO) in 1926 have been transferred to Davos. Arosa is located approximately 12 km south-west of Davos and about 200 m higher. The parallel observation period (with instruments of Arosa being temporarily deployed in Davos) started on 22 Nov 2011. 4 of the 6 instruments (2 Brewers 2 Dobsons) were definitively transferred to Davos on 28 Feb 2018. The two remaining instruments (1 Brewer and 1 Dobson) continued observations in Arosa until 18 Feb 2021 when they were also transferred to Davos and the ozone observations at Arosa definitively stopped. Since then, the ozone time series is continued from Davos.
